event: The Immaculate Conception of the Virgin Mary
symbols: crescent moon beneath Mary's feet — from Revelation 12:1, the woman clothed with the sun with the moon under her feet, triumph over mutability
circle of twelve stars above her head — the crown of twelve stars from Revelation 12, representing the fullness of God's people
hands clasped in prayer — Mary's perfect devotion, the handmaid of the Lord whose fiat made the incarnation possible
white robe — purity and sinlessness, the immaculate state preserved from all stain of original sin
dark blue mantle — the heavenly origin of Mary's vocation, royalty and depth of faith
cherubim surrounding Mary — the angelic court attending the one chosen to bear the Son of God
scroll held by a cherub — a scriptural text affirming Mary's prerogatives in salvation history
description: The Virgin Mary stands on a crescent moon atop clouds, surrounded by cherubim. She wears a white robe beneath a dark blue-green mantle. Her long brown hair falls over her shoulders and her hands are pressed in prayer. Above her head twelve small stars form a luminous crown with golden light radiating against a warm amber sky. Below, five or six cherubs cluster around her feet and the cloud bank. One on the right holds a scroll with inscribed text. Warm golden-brown tones dominate alongside the white gown and deep blue mantle.
theological_description: Murillo's 'Immaculate Conception' is a supreme visual statement of Marian theology. The doctrine that Mary was preserved from original sin from the moment of her conception finds its visual vocabulary in Revelation 12:1: 'A great sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet, and on her head a crown of twelve stars.' The crescent moon, stellar crown, and radiant light follow this apocalyptic vision precisely. The theological roots extend deeper: Mary standing on the moon connects to Genesis 3:15, the protoevangelium promising the seed of the woman would crush the serpent's head. Mary is the new Eve whose obedience reverses the old Eve's disobedience, as the Church Fathers taught. Her prayerful posture recalls the Magnificat (Luke 1:46-55), declaring that God regarded the humble estate of his servant. The cherubim echo the Annunciation (Luke 1:26-38): as Gabriel declared her 'full of grace,' these heavenly beings witness her unique status. The scroll likely references Song of Solomon 4:7 applied to Mary: 'You are altogether beautiful, my love; there is no flaw in you.' The luminous palette is itself theological: a being in whom darkness has no part, the vessel prepared to bear the Light of the World.

scripture_refs.reason: A woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet and twelve stars crowning her — the direct visual source for Immaculate Conception iconography
Hail, full of grace, the Lord is with you — the angelic greeting declaring Mary's unique status, theological basis for the Immaculate Conception
The Magnificat — Mary's prayer of praise declaring God has done great things for her, embodied in her prayerful posture
The seed of the woman shall crush the serpent's head — the protoevangelium identifying Mary as the new Eve
You are altogether beautiful, my love; there is no flaw in you — traditionally applied to Mary as preserved from all stain of sin
Blessed are you among women — Elizabeth's Spirit-filled recognition of Mary's singular blessedness
The virgin shall conceive and bear a son — prophetic foundation for the theology of Mary's perpetual purity
